DE.name: Germany

notes: Alphanumeric sender ID widely supported — your brand name is the sender
No long codes available — use alphanumeric or shortcode
Content must comply with UWG (Unfair Competition Act)
Opt-in must be double opt-in (DOI) for marketing
gotchas: Marketing SMS allowed Mon–Sat 07:00–21:00 only — outside hours messages may be filtered
URL shorteners (bit.ly etc.) increasingly blocked by carriers — use your own domain
GDPR consent records must be stored and producible on request

notes: WhatsApp penetration in Germany is very high (~80% of smartphone users)
BSP (Business Solution Provider) required — e.g. Messente
Marketing templates must be pre-approved by Meta
24-hour customer service window for replies
gotchas: Meta can reject templates without detailed explanation — build buffer time
German users have high privacy expectations — generic marketing copy performs poorly
Phone number must be verified and cannot be used simultaneously in WhatsApp app

notes: Strong carrier support: Deutsche Telekom, Vodafone DE, O2 all support RCS
Verified sender badge builds strong brand trust
Rich cards and carousels supported
gotchas: Android-only until full cross-platform adoption — SMS fallback essential
Agent (sender) registration involves Google + each carrier — timeline varies

DE.market_notes: Germany is one of Europe's largest messaging markets. WhatsApp is dominant for personal use. Germans are privacy-conscious — personalised, relevant messaging outperforms broadcast. Double opt-in is legally expected, not optional.

FR.name: France

notes: No long codes — alphanumeric or shortcodes only
Shortcodes have minimum 3-month term, 3-month notice to return
OACP (Operateur d'Acces au Contenu et aux Services de Proximité) registration required for shortcodes
STOP must be written in French: 'STOP' keyword required, CONTACT keyword must return company name + support
gotchas: Marketing SMS BLOCKED on Sundays, public holidays, and after 22:00 — messages queued, not dropped
URL shorteners (bit.ly, tinyurl) strictly FORBIDDEN by ARCEP — use your own branded short domain
Carrier freeze periods in August and December — no new registrations processed
Inactive shortcodes (30 days no traffic) risk withdrawal by carrier

notes: WhatsApp used by ~60% of French smartphone users
GDPR applies — explicit consent required
gotchas: French consumers use WhatsApp less than Germany or Spain — SMS/RCS often higher reach
Avoid registering numbers during August — carrier and Meta support slows significantly

notes: Orange reported 400% RCS traffic increase in 2024
Strong carrier RCS support across all major French operators
gotchas: RCS sending time restrictions mirror SMS rules — no Sunday/holiday marketing
Agent registration through French carriers adds time vs other EU markets

compliance.framework: GDPR + LCEN + Code de la Consommation

FR.market_notes: France has strict SMS timing rules and unique registration bureaucracy. Plan for August and December freezes. URL shorteners are banned — this catches almost everyone. Budget 6–8 weeks for full shortcode setup.

PL.name: Poland

notes: One of the more accessible EU markets — no mandatory pre-registration for alphanumeric
Long codes available, unlike France/Germany
GDPR applies — consent records required
gotchas: Polish carriers increasingly filter unregistered alphanumeric IDs — voluntary registration recommended
Marketing hours: weekdays 06:00–22:00, weekends 08:00–22:00
Polish consumers have low spam tolerance — high unsubscribe rates if messaging is not relevant

notes: WhatsApp widely used in Poland — strong alternative to SMS for marketing
gotchas: Template approval can be slow for Polish-language templates — submit early

notes: RCS not yet widely supported by Polish carriers as of 2025 — check for updates
gotchas: Do not plan RCS campaigns in Poland without verifying carrier support

PL.market_notes: Poland is one of the most accessible EU markets for SMS. Relatively lower costs, good deliverability. RCS is immature — SMS and WhatsApp are the channels to focus on.

GB.name: United Kingdom

notes: All UK long codes require an approved RC (Regulatory Compliance) bundle and KYC since May 2024
International long codes to UK numbers are universally blocked since mid-2024
Alphanumeric sender ID well-supported for one-way traffic
gotchas: CRITICAL: International long codes (non-UK numbers) are BLOCKED — must use UK-local number or alphanumeric
RC bundle approval required before purchasing new UK numbers — budget 1–2 weeks
ICO registration required if processing personal data — £52/year fee
PECR (Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations) applies on top of UK GDPR

notes: WhatsApp used by ~60% of UK adults
Strong channel for customer support and transactional messages
gotchas: UK users can be skeptical of WhatsApp business messages — trust-building copy is essential

notes: UK has strong RCS carrier support across all four major networks
Verified sender builds trust in a market concerned about fraud
gotchas: Ofcom anti-fraud rules mean strict template review — avoid anything that looks like a bank alert without verification

GB.market_notes: Post-Brexit the UK maintains GDPR-equivalent rules via UK GDPR. The big gotcha is the international long code block — almost every new sender gets caught by this. RCS is mature and a good choice for brand-safety-conscious UK enterprises.

US.name: United States

notes: Alphanumeric sender IDs NOT supported in the US — use 10DLC, toll-free, or shortcode
10DLC (10-Digit Long Code) is the standard for A2P: register brand + campaign on TCR
Toll-free numbers require separate verification — good for low-to-medium volume
Shortcodes cost $500–$1000/month but highest throughput and deliverability
gotchas: SHAFT content (Sex, Hate, Alcohol, Firearms, Tobacco) will get your entire campaign binding blocked — no exceptions
TCPA violations carry $500–$1500 per unsolicited message — class action risk is real
TCR registration does not guarantee delivery — carrier-level vetting is separate
Artificially slow traffic if sending at high volume — carriers enforce throughput limits per 10DLC

notes: Meta PAUSED marketing-category WhatsApp messages to US phone numbers since April 1 2025
Utility and authentication templates still work for US numbers
Do not build a US marketing campaign on WhatsApp — use SMS or RCS
gotchas: This pause may be permanent or extended — do not plan around WhatsApp for US marketing

notes: Apple added RCS support in iOS 18 — US market now has near-universal RCS reach
AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile all support RCS
Strong alternative to WhatsApp for US marketing given Meta pause
gotchas: RCS agent registration involves Google review + carrier approval — budget 4–6 weeks
No SHAFT content rules carry over to RCS — same content restrictions apply

compliance.framework: TCPA + CAN-SPAM + State laws (CCPA for CA)

US.market_notes: The US is the highest-volume market but most legally complex. TCPA class actions are a real business risk. Never send without express written consent. 10DLC is mandatory for A2P. WhatsApp marketing is currently blocked for US numbers — plan for SMS + RCS.

IN.name: India

notes: TRAI DLT registration is MANDATORY — all entities, headers, and templates must be pre-registered
Register on one of: JioConnect, Vodafone DLT, Airtel DLT, or BSNL DLT
Every single message template must be pre-approved — no ad hoc messages
Three content categories: transactional, promotional, service
gotchas: DLT registration is the most complex in Asia — budget 2–3 weeks minimum
Promotional SMS blocked 21:00–09:00 daily, no exceptions
National Do Not Disturb (NDNC) registry must be checked — violations carry heavy fines
Template modifications require re-approval — build this buffer into campaign planning
Commercial messages restricted to specific time windows per TRAI rules

notes: WhatsApp is the dominant messaging platform in India — 500M+ users
Very cost-effective for marketing vs SMS
Rich media and interactive templates perform extremely well
gotchas: Indian WhatsApp users report spam aggressively — quality messaging essential
Business verification requires Indian business documentation

notes: Growing RCS support — Airtel and Jio have significant RCS deployments
RCS can bypass some DLT complexity for certain use cases
gotchas: Carrier RCS support is uneven — always have SMS DLT fallback in place

IN.market_notes: India has the most complex SMS registration in the world (DLT). However, WhatsApp is transformative here — the most cost-effective way to reach Indian consumers at scale. Budget 3 weeks for DLT; 2 weeks for WhatsApp. Never skip NDNC checks.

BR.name: Brazil

notes: Alphanumeric sender IDs not supported — local long codes or shortcodes
LGPD (Brazil's GDPR equivalent) applies — consent records required
SMS open rates high but spam filtering increasing
gotchas: Brazilian carriers aggressively filter A2P traffic — local number strongly preferred
Marketing hours: 08:00–20:00 local time — no weekend evening sends
ANATEL (regulator) has been increasing enforcement

notes: Brazil is one of WhatsApp's largest markets — 120M+ users, ~90% smartphone penetration
WhatsApp is the PRIMARY business communication channel in Brazil
WhatsApp Pay is active — commerce integration possible
gotchas: Brazilian consumers expect fast, conversational WhatsApp replies — slow response = blocked
Meta Business Verification may require Brazilian CNPJ business registration

notes: RCS growing in Brazil — Vivo and Claro have active RCS deployments
gotchas: WhatsApp so dominant in Brazil that RCS ROI is lower — assess per use case

BR.market_notes: Brazil = WhatsApp first, always. WhatsApp is how Brazilians communicate with businesses. SMS is secondary — useful for OTP and alerts but not the primary marketing channel. If you only do one thing in Brazil, set up WhatsApp Business.

ES.name: Spain

notes: No long codes — alphanumeric or shortcodes
Relatively straightforward setup vs France/Germany
GDPR applies
gotchas: CNMC (regulator) can impose high fines for GDPR violations
Marketing hours: 09:00–21:00 weekdays, shorter on weekends
Spanish carriers increasing spam filtering — content quality matters

notes: WhatsApp penetration in Spain is among the highest in Europe (~90%)
Primary messaging app for both personal and business communication
gotchas: Spanish users are highly active on WhatsApp — high expectations for response speed

notes: Good carrier RCS support in Spain
gotchas: WhatsApp dominance means RCS reach is lower — test before scaling

ES.market_notes: Spain is one of the easiest EU markets to launch in — simpler registration than France or Germany, high WhatsApp penetration. Good starting market for EU expansion. WhatsApp is the dominant channel.

SE.name: Sweden

notes: Swedish market is relatively simple — no mandatory pre-registration for alphanumeric
High smartphone penetration, tech-literate consumers
GDPR applies
gotchas: IMY (Privacy regulator) is among EU's most active GDPR enforcers
Swedish consumers have very low spam tolerance — relevance and personalisation essential

notes: WhatsApp used but iMessage and native SMS are also very strong in Sweden
B2C WhatsApp is less dominant than in Southern Europe
gotchas: Swedes are skeptical of business WhatsApp messages — SMS or RCS may outperform

notes: Strong RCS carrier support in Sweden — Telia was early adopter

SE.market_notes: Sweden is a clean, uncomplicated market to launch in. Low friction, high tech adoption. RCS performs well. IMY is aggressive on GDPR — document consent properly.

NL.name: Netherlands

notes: One of the most flexible EU markets — long codes, shortcodes, and alphanumeric all available
AWS expanded self-service registration to Netherlands in 2025
GDPR applies
gotchas: AP (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) is active GDPR enforcer
Telemarketing Do Not Call register (Bel-me-niet register) applies to voice — check for SMS equivalent

notes: WhatsApp widely used in Netherlands
Good RCS support across Dutch carriers

NL.market_notes: Netherlands is a strong, flexible market. Good mix of channels available. English is widely spoken — less localisation friction than France or Germany.

AU.name: Australia

notes: ACMA introduced Sender ID registration in 2023 to combat scam SMS
Unregistered sender IDs increasingly blocked by Australian carriers
AWS added self-service Australia registration in 2025
gotchas: Spam Act 2003 carries fines up to AUD$2.2M per day for non-compliance
Must include a functional unsubscribe mechanism in every marketing message
Registered sender IDs get priority delivery — unregistered traffic is deprioritised

notes: WhatsApp less dominant in Australia than EU/LATAM — iMessage and SMS are stronger
gotchas: Low WhatsApp penetration relative to population — verify audience before investing

notes: Growing RCS adoption in Australia — Telstra has active RCS deployment

AU.market_notes: Australia has one of the highest compliance cost environments. The Spam Act has real teeth — AUD$2.2M/day fines are enforced. Sender ID registration is now effectively mandatory. Strong SMS market despite high costs.

SG.name: Singapore

notes: Singapore launched mandatory SSIR in 2023 — all sender IDs must be registered
Unregistered sender IDs displayed as 'Likely-SCAM' by carriers
IMDA oversees the registry — high enforcement
gotchas: CRITICAL: Unregistered sender IDs flagged as SCAM — this will destroy campaign performance
PDPA requires explicit opt-in for marketing messages
Singapore is a regional hub — many brands send from SG to broader APAC
Registration processing: 3–5 business days typically

notes: WhatsApp widely used in Singapore — strong business messaging channel
Singapore has strong RCS support — tech-forward market

SG.market_notes: Singapore is the gateway to APAC. Strict but fast registration (SSIR). A tech-forward, wealthy market with strong RCS and WhatsApp adoption. If you're expanding in Asia, Singapore is often the first stop.

FI.name: Finland

notes: Traficom maintains a protected sender ID list — if your name matches, LOA required
Gambling and lottery content is STRICTLY PROHIBITED — not just restricted
AWS added self-service Finland registration in 2025
GDPR + Finnish Information Society Code applies
gotchas: If your brand name matches a protected name on Traficom's list, you need a Letter of Authority AND a Numbering Decision — adds weeks
Absolutely no gambling/lottery content — this is a hard ban, not a licensing issue

notes: WhatsApp used but SMS and native messaging stronger in Finland
Good RCS support in Finland

FI.market_notes: Finland is a small but high-value market. Traficom is strict — check the protected sender name list before starting. Gambling content is completely banned. Otherwise a straightforward Nordic market.

IT.name: Italy

notes: No mandatory pre-registration but carrier filtering is aggressive
AWS added self-service Italy registration in 2025 — recommended to register
GDPR applies + Garante Privacy (one of EU's most active regulators)
gotchas: Garante has issued major GDPR fines — consent documentation is essential
Marketing hours: 09:00–20:00 Monday–Saturday only
No Sunday marketing SMS
Italian carriers filter aggressively — content quality and registered IDs important

notes: WhatsApp widely used in Italy — strong business messaging channel
gotchas: Italian businesses often prefer voice calls — WhatsApp messaging requires cultural calibration

notes: AWS added Italy to self-service RCS/SMS in 2025 — improving

IT.market_notes: Italy is a high-value market with an active privacy regulator (Garante). Strict marketing hours. WhatsApp is strong. The Garante issues meaningful fines — treat consent as seriously as in Germany.

DK.name: Denmark

notes: Registration since April 7, 2025 is mandatory — unregistered IDs blocked
Sender IDs cannot contain 'TEST', 'MESSAGE', 'SMS', or carrier names
AWS added self-service Denmark registration in 2025
gotchas: CRITICAL: Registration became mandatory April 7 2025 — unregistered traffic is now blocked
Forbidden words in sender IDs: TEST, MESSAGE, SMS, carrier names (SMART, SUN etc.)
Shortened URLs prohibited in Denmark

notes: WhatsApp used but SMS and native messaging also strong
Good Nordic RCS support

DK.market_notes: Denmark went mandatory on SMS sender registration in April 2025 — a recent change that many international senders miss. Do not attempt unregistered alphanumeric IDs. Small but high-value market.
